title: ornithogalum orthophyllum subsp. psammophilum
intro: Ornithogalum orthophyllum subsp. psammophilum, also known as the desert star or star-of-Bethlehem, is a bulbous perennial plant native to South Africa. The plant is used in traditional medicine, where its bulbs contain medicinal properties such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ornithogalum orthophyllum subsp. psammophilum include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the bulbs are typically dried and used as a decoction or infusion to treat various ailments, although modern research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
